ingredients.item: 1 cup (50 g) loosely packed fresh mint leaves
ingredients.original: 1 cup (50 g) loosely packed fresh mint leaves

ingredients.item: 1 Add the water to a stovetop teapot (or a saucepan) with a strainer then add the loose-leaf tea to the strainer. Place the teapot over medium-high heat and bring to a boil.
ingredients.original: 1 Add the water to a stovetop teapot (or a saucepan) with a strainer then add the loose-leaf tea to the strainer. Place the teapot over medium-high heat and bring to a boil.

ingredients.item: 2 Once boiling, reduce the heat to medium-low and let simmer for 5 minutes. After 5 minutes, and after you smell the tea strongly, turn off the heat.
ingredients.original: 2 Once boiling, reduce the heat to medium-low and let simmer for 5 minutes. After 5 minutes, and after you smell the tea strongly, turn off the heat.

ingredients.item: 3 Boil a kettle of water to serve on the side. The water can be used to reduce the strength of the tea for those who like it less strong.
ingredients.original: 3 Boil a kettle of water to serve on the side. The water can be used to reduce the strength of the tea for those who like it less strong.

ingredients.item: 4 Place some of the mint leaves in each teacup, then pour the tea into the teacups.
ingredients.original: 4 Place some of the mint leaves in each teacup, then pour the tea into the teacups.
